fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: Fast-growing software company with strong revenue expansion and positive free cash flow, but currently unprofitable on a net basis and carrying significant debt relative to equity.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 11.5%; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow $463.32M TTM — generating cash from operations.
Forward P/E 43.64x — reasonable for a growth software name.
negatives: Profit margin -2.9% — currently loss-making on a net basis.
Debt/Equity 2.13 — high leverage relative to equity base.
Earnings growth 2.2% YoY — lagging revenue growth.

news.paragraph: MongoDB reported Q1 earnings that beat estimates, with EPS of $1.32 versus an expected $1.18, and also saw its revenue grow 26.7% year-over-year. Despite the positive earnings surprise and analyst price target raises from Barclays and Wedbush, the stock saw a muted reaction, pausing after recent gains. The company's AI initiatives were noted as a positive driver, but the market appears to be digesting the results ahead of the next earnings date.

headlines.title: MongoDB Beats Earnings, But Shares Hit Pause Despite AI Gains - MongoDB (NASDAQ:MDB)
